channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: BYU loses in first round of NCAA Tournament
item.description: WEST LAFAYETTE, Ind. — The BYU women's volleyball team was making its 13th consecutive appearance in the NCAA Tournament. But for the first time since 2005, the Cougars are going home after losing in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The A's clear the last major hurdle for a $1.75 billion Las Vegas stadium
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The Las Vegas Stadium Authority approved lease, non-relocation and development documents Thursday to clear the last major hurdles for the Athletics to construct a $1.75 billion stadium on the Strip and bring Major League Baseball to the expanding sports market.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Manny Pacquiao, winner of titles in a record 8 weight classes, elected to Boxing Hall of Fame
item.description: Manny Pacquiao, who won titles in a record eight weight classes from flyweight to super welterweight while becoming one of boxing's biggest draws, was elected Thursday to the International Boxing Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina interviews Bill Belichick for head coaching job, AP sources say
item.description: North Carolina has interviewed former New England Patriots coach and six-time Super Bowl champion Bill Belichick for its head coaching position, two people with knowledge of the situation said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tottenham joins list of top Premier League teams to lose at Bournemouth as fans jeer Postecoglou
item.description: Manchester City, Arsenal, and now Tottenham. The list of top Premier League teams beaten at Bournemouth this season is growing. Dean Huijsen took advantage of Tottenham’s weakness at set pieces to head home a 17th-minute winner in Bournemouth’s 1-0 victory on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Scottie Scheffler has new putting grip and trails Cameron Young by 3 in Bahamas
item.description: NASSAU, Bahamas (AP) — Scottie Scheffler brought a new putting grip to the Hero World Challenge and felt enough improvement to be satisfied with the result, a 5-under 67 that left him three shots behind Cameron Young on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jack Eichel says Team USA seeks to prove it has closed the gap on Canada at 4 Nations Face-Off
item.description: It didn’t take much to stir Jack Eichel’s national pride in looking ahead to representing the United States at the 4 Nations Face-Off tournament. Appearing on a Team USA Zoom call on Thursday, the Vegas Golden Knights forward jumped right in when reminded how U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Urban Meyer shuts down Ohio State return rumors, fully backs head coach Ryan Day
item.description: Urban Meyer will "always be a Buckeye," but the former head coach said Thursday he has no plans to return with Ryan Day facing scrutiny after another loss to Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amber Glenn takes the lead in Grand Prix Final figure skating after Kaori Sakamoto falls
item.description: GRENOBLE, France (AP) — Competing with five Japanese skaters in the six-woman field, Amber Glenn of the United States continued her breakout season in figure skating by taking the lead in the short program at the Grand Prix Final on Thursday as world champion Kaori Sakamoto fell.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Total number of Texas fans caught and punished for throwing bottles in Georgia game: Zero
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The University of Texas investigation into the bottle-throwing incident that disrupted the Texas-Georgia game in October — and drew a harsh rebuke and fine from the Southeastern Conference — resulted in no one being caught or punished.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Batty, Ferrin named to All-Big 12 first team
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Three Cougars were named first-team All-Big 12 including defensive end Tyler Batty, offensive left tackle Caleb Etienne and kicker Will Ferrin. In addition, senior defensive back Jakob Robinson and junior kick returner Keelan Marion earned All-Big 12 Second Team honors.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says it 'ridiculous' to believe he has more to prove, still hopes to return to Jets next year
item.description: While Aaron Rodgers remains interested in a future with the Jets, it is unclear if the franchise has mutual feelings about the relationship continuing in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former MLB pitcher Ben McDonald shares how emotional moment while hunting led to big buck kill
item.description: Former MLB top draft pick Ben McDonald revealed in a social media post how an emotional moment thinking about his father led to bagging a big buck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Holy Score: Brigham Young’s fate in conference race, playoff rankings should prompt Big 12 to reassess its football strategy
item.description: The Cougars have a better resume than either of the teams playing for the conference championship, Arizona State and Iowa State.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: George Russell accuses Max Verstappen of bullying and threatening behavior as F1 feud deepens
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Mercedes driver George Russell has accused Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen of bullying and threatening behavior as a dispute between the two at last week's race in Qatar deepened Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Creighton makes it 2 wins in less than a year against No. 1s, with Jayhawks latest to lose in Omaha
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Creighton has played eight No. 1 teams in its basketball history, and the first six of those games weren't close. All were losses. The last two? Bluejays blowouts at the CHI Health Center.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Providence College AD apologizes for offensive religious chant toward BYU basketball team
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- The athletic director at Providence College -- an East Coast catholic university -- apologized to Brigham Young University on social media for offensive chants made by the student section during Tuesday's men's basketball game.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Cougars go cold in 83-64 loss at Providence
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team suffered its worst shooting performance of the season and lost at Providence Tuesday night, 83-64 to fall to 6-2 on the season. The Cougars shot just 33.3 percent from the field in scoring a season-low 64 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois Football assistant coach dies at age 62
item.description: CHAMPAIGN, Ill. (WCIA) -- Dana Dimel, the Senior Offensive Assistant for the Illinois Fighting Illini football team, has died. Illinois Athletics announced that the longtime coach passed away Tuesday at the age of 62. He is survived by his wife Julie and their children Winston and Joey.
item.dc:creator: Bradley Zimmerman, WGN 9

item.title: McCaffrey injury potentially rules him out for rest of season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: CU Buffs await bowl selection
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—The Colorado Buffaloes won't be heading to the Big 12 title game, but they're still sitting comfortably in the AP Top 25 at No. 20. With nine wins, including seven in conference play, it's a season most Buffs fans would call a success. Now, it's a waiting game.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Philadelphia defense, Tucker's misses help the Eagles to a 24-19 win in their showdown with Ravens
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Saquon Barkley vs. Derrick Henry was never supposed to be a head-to-head matchup. “It ain't basketball. It ain't like I go out there and tackle Derrick Henry,” Barkley said. “To be honest, I don't know if I really want to tackle Derrick Henry.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Monahan with goal and assist, Blue Jackets extend points streak to 6 with 6-3 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Sean Monahan had a goal and assist, and the Columbus Blue Jackets extended their points streak to six games with a 6-3 win over the Chicago Blackhawks on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 6 Chicagoland prep football programs bring home IHSA state championships over the weekend in Normal
item.description: Six Chicagoland prep football programs, including two from the city, claimed Illinois High School Association state championships over Thanksgiving weekend in Normal.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: BYU eliminated from Big 12 contention, beats Houston
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU learned it was eliminated from Big 12 Championship Game contention at halftime, but the Cougars still had a job to do. Jake Retzlaff ran for two touchdowns and threw for 167 yards to lead No. 19 BYU to a 30-18 victory over Houston on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kyrie Irving scores 30 to lead Dallas past Jazz, 106-94
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Kyrie Irving went for 30 points, as the Dallas Mavericks beat the Utah Jazz for a second time this season, 106-94.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, WGN 9

item.title: Utes close disappointing season with 28-14 victory
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (ABC4 Sports) - Utah forced three turnovers and scored two defensive touchdowns Friday night, including a 60-yard pick-6 by Zemaiah Vaughn, as the Utes snapped a seven-game losing streak with a 28-14 victory over UCF.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Aggies blow 17-point fourth quarter lead in loss to CSU
item.description: FORT COLLINS, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State football team could not hold a 30-13 fourth quarter lead in its season finale at Colorado State, and the Aggies end the season with a 42-37 loss to the Rams.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers extend contract of postseason hero
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have agreed to a contract extension with one of the key pieces of the team's World Series title run. The Dodgers reached agreement Friday on a deal with Tommy Edman, ensuring that the do-it-all utility-man will remain with the team for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Spurgeon breaks 33-game scoreless streak with two goals, Minnesota beats Chicago 3-2
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Jared Spurgeon ended a 33-game scoring drought with two of Minnesota’s three second-period goals and the Wild beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Friday. Marco Rossi also scored for Minnesota and Marc-Andre Fleury, who turned 40 on Thursday, made 20 saves to improve to 5-0-1.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9
